core_payload.content:
# Background Agent Runtime

## 1. Role
Background Agent Runtime provides the durable substrate for long-lived agent work across N1, A2C, Symphony, N-Infinity, and Vault Steward lanes while keeping gating, replay, recovery, and operator trust explicit. It routes that substrate through four bounded sub-hubs so lifecycle, control, mutation authority, and execution orchestration do not collapse into one flat doctrine surface.

## 2. Routing Surfaces
- `daemon-lifecycle` governs host bring-up, sleep posture, night windows, model readiness, and cost-aware continuity.
- `control-plane` governs assignment continuity, memory state, instruction surfaces, context packs, harness semantics, and operator-visible controls.
- `mutation-gates` governs permission-to-run, workspace profiling, intake risk review, routing discipline, validation, and critique pressure.
- `work-orchestration` governs job envelopes, delegation, observability, stewardship maintenance, and mining or update workers.

## 3. Parent Boundary
The parent runtime hub keeps only top-level relationships to N1Hub, Personal AI Assistant, human-ai symbiosis, the enclosing project capsules, To Dig Deep, and the four sub-hubs. Detailed runtime atoms route through the sub-hubs so the autonomy substrate stays legible, replayable, and reviewable.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Background Agent Runtime is the parent hub for four bounded runtime surfaces: daemon lifecycle, control plane, mutation gates, and work orchestration. The parent keeps only top-level relationships to N1Hub, Personal AI Assistant, human-ai symbiosis, and its project envelopes while the detailed runtime atoms route through sub-hubs. This keeps long-lived autonomy legible, replayable, and reviewable, and it prevents lifecycle logic, control continuity, mutation authority, and worker orchestration from collapsing into one flat doctrine surface.
